summary.intent: Implement a framework-agnostic Clipboard core component under `ars-components::specialized::clipboard`, covering state transitions, typed effect intents, connect API attrs, spec-conformance anatomy, and snapshots, then open PR #686 and address Codex review findings.
summary.outcome: PR #686 was opened, Codex review triggered and completed with 3 threads, all findings addressed (removed conflicting `aria-labelledby` from trigger attrs, added regression test, refreshed snapshots, fixed missing `Vec` import for no_std), and changes committed and pushed.

repo: The repo uses a `waiting-for-codex-review` skill loop at `.agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md` that must be run after first PR push; it polls for Codex 👀/👍 reactions and unresolved threads via GitHub GraphQL.
Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment; active review is signaled by the 👀 reaction, approval by 👍.
Snapshot tests use `cargo insta` with `--unreferenced=ignore` flag; trigger snapshots live under `crates/ars-components/src/specialized/clipboard/snapshots/`.
The repo enforces nightly rustfmt (`cargo +nightly fmt --all --check`) and spec validation (`cargo xtask spec validate`) as part of the PR verification checklist.
PRs that add `.snap` files require the `snapshot-reviewed` label applied via `gh pr edit <n> --add-label snapshot-reviewed`.
The accessible-name contract for the clipboard trigger uses only a dynamic state-specific `aria-label`; combining `aria-label` with `aria-labelledby` violates ARIA spec and was flagged by Codex.
The Clipboard module operates under `no_std` constraints; `Vec` and `vec!` must be explicitly imported from the appropriate no_std-compatible source.

code.finding: trigger_attrs function originally included both `aria-label` and `aria-labelledby`, creating conflicting accessible name sources; fix removes `aria-labelledby` entirely and relies solely on dynamic state-specific `aria-label`.
Missing `Vec` import caused a no_std compile error; importing it explicitly resolved the issue without affecting default-features builds.
Trigger attrs section in the spec was updated to remove `aria-labelledby` reference, keeping spec and implementation in sync.

workflow: The Codex review polling loop exits on 👍 (approval) or when unresolved Codex threads appear without 👀 (needs action); normal review for a larger PR can take 4+ minutes in the 👀 state.
Cargo `--no-default-features` check failures in unrelated existing modules should not block Clipboard-specific verification; scope errors by file path before concluding the fix is incomplete.
rustfmt can reformat long test assertions added during review fixes; always re-run `cargo +nightly fmt --all --check` after adding new test code and before committing.

friction: `cargo check -p ars-components --lib --no-default-features` reported failures in unrelated existing modules, making it ambiguous whether the Clipboard-specific `Vec` error was resolved; required grepping output for the specific file path to confirm.
Codex review took 4+ minutes of active polling before producing findings, with multiple silent polling cycles before threads appeared.
rustfmt reformatted a newly added long assertion after the first commit attempt, requiring an extra fmt + recheck + rerun cycle before the final commit.
open_items: Existing `no_std` failures in other modules (not Clipboard) surfaced during `--no-default-features` check but were not addressed in this session.
The `cargo mutants` and `cargo llvm-cov` validation steps listed in the PR description were not run in the transcript; coverage and mutation scores for the Clipboard core are unverified.
